key_insight: A two-stage evolutionary search can make PINN training more reliable by first cheaply screening hyperparameters with short runs and then fully training only the most promising configurations.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to improve PDE-solving PINNs under fixed compute budgets, especially when training is unstable and hyperparameter sensitivity is high.
method: Use an evolutionary algorithm to explore mixed, black-box hyperparameter spaces.
Run low-fidelity, truncated training epochs to quickly rank candidate PINN settings.
Fully train the top candidates with standard gradient-based optimization in a second stage.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on Advection, Klein-Gordon, and Helmholtz benchmarks and reportedly achieved lower mean error than standard training under constrained computational resources.
watch_item: The extra outer-loop search adds implementation complexity and may still depend on careful budget allocation between screening and full training.
paper_title: Evolutionary Two-Stage Hyperparameter Optimization Strategies for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
